briefdescription.para: Pre focus change signal.
Focus changed signal.
Focus group changed signal.
Creates a FocusManager handle; this can be initialized with FocusManager::New().

detaileddescription.para: Calling member functions with an uninitialized handle is not allowed.

briefdescription.para: Destructor.

detaileddescription.para: This is non-virtual since derived Handle types must not contain data or virtual methods.

briefdescription.para: Moves the focus to the given view.

detaileddescription.para: Only one view can be focused at the same time. The view must be in the stage already and focusable.

simplesect.para: Whether the focus is successful or not
The FocusManager has been initialized.
The View has been initialized.
If the parent of this view has the KEYBOARD FOCUSABLE CHILDREN property set to false, it will not be focused.

briefdescription.para: Gets the current focused view.

simplesect.para: A handle to the current focused view or an empty handle if no view is focused
The FocusManager has been initialized.

briefdescription.para: Moves the focus to the next focusable actor in the focus chain in the given direction (according to the focus traversal order).

simplesect.para: true if the movement was successful
The FocusManager has been initialized.

briefdescription.para: Clears the focus from the current focused actor if any, so that no actor is focused in the focus chain.

detaileddescription.para:
It will emit focus changed signal without current focused actor.
The FocusManager has been initialized.

briefdescription.para: Sets whether the focus movement should be looped within the same focus group.

detaileddescription.para:
The focus movement is not looped by default.
enabled
Whether the focus movement should be looped
The FocusManager has been initialized.

briefdescription.para: Gets whether the focus movement should be looped within the same focus group.

simplesect.para: Whether the focus movement should be looped
The FocusManager has been initialized.

briefdescription.para: Sets whether a view is a focus group that can limit the scope of focus movement to its child views in the focus chain.

detaileddescription.para: Layout views set themselves as focus groups by default.

parameterdescription.para: The view to be set as a focus group
Whether to set the view as a focus group or not

simplesect.para: The FocusManager has been initialized.
The View has been initialized.

briefdescription.para: Checks whether the view is set as a focus group or not.

simplesect.para: Whether the view is set as a focus group
The FocusManager has been initialized.
The View has been initialized.

briefdescription.para: Returns the closest ancestor of the given view that is a focus group.

parameterdescription.para: The view to be checked for its focus group

simplesect.para: The focus group the given view belongs to or an empty handle if the given view doesn't belong to any focus group

briefdescription.para: Sets the focus indicator actor.

detaileddescription.para: This will replace the default focus indicator actor in FocusManager and will be added to the focused actor as a highlight.

parameterdescription.para: The indicator view to be added

simplesect.para: The FocusManager has been initialized.
The indicator view has been initialized.

briefdescription.para: Gets the focus indicator view.

simplesect.para: A handle to the focus indicator view
The FocusManager has been initialized.

briefdescription.para: Move the focus to prev focused actor.
Gets the device of the last focus change.

detaileddescription.para: This method returns what caused the most recent focus change, allowing applications to differentiate between different input methods.

simplesect.para: The device of the last focus change
The FocusManager has been initialized.

briefdescription.para: Gets the device name that caused the last focus change.

detaileddescription.para: This method returns the name of the input device that caused the most recent focus change. For non-device inputs (like programmatic focus changes), an empty string may be returned.

simplesect.para: The device name that caused the last focus change
The FocusManager has been initialized.

briefdescription.para: Sets whether to clear focus when window loses focus.

detaileddescription.para: By default, this is enabled. When disabled, the focus state is preserved even when the window loses focus,

parameterdescription.para: Whether to clear focus when window loses focus

briefdescription.para: Gets whether to clear focus when window loses focus.

simplesect.para: Whether clear focus is enabled when window loses focus

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted before the focus is going to be changed.

detaileddescription.para: FocusManager makes the best guess for which view to focus towards the given direction, but applications might want to change that. By connecting with this signal, they can check the proposed view to focus and return a different view if they wish. This signal is only emitted when the navigation key is pressed and FocusManager tries to move the focus automatically. It won't be emitted for focus movement by calling SetCurrentFocusView directly.

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted after the current focused view has been changed.
This signal is emitted when the focus group has been changed.

detaileddescription.para: If the current focus group has a parent layout view, FocusManager will make the best guess for the next focus group to move the focus to in the given direction (forward or backward). If not, the application has to set the new focus.

briefdescription.para: Gets the singleton of FocusManager object.

simplesect.para: A handle to the FocusManager view

briefdescription.para: Provides the functionality of handling focus navigation and maintaining the two dimensional focus chain.

detaileddescription.para: It provides functionality of setting the focus and moving the focus in four directions (i.e. Left, Right, Up and Down). It also draws a highlight for the focused view and emits a signal when the focus is changed.

Signals
Signal Name
Method
preFocusChange
PreFocusChangeSignal()
focusChanged
FocusChangedSignal()
focusGroupChanged
FocusGroupChangedSignal()
